note: Canonical contract for the super-admin global generation settings. Single source of truth: functions/api validates + serves this; the React panel renders label/help/tradeoff/min/max/recommended from it. The Python generators do NOT read this file — they apply settings.get(key, <existing constant>), and those constants equal the defaults below, so an empty app_settings row is a no-op.

light_model.label: Model (Light)
light_model.help: Which Claude model the single-call Light engine uses.
light_model.tradeoff: Opus produces the richest prose and scoring but costs 3-5x and is slower. Sonnet is the balanced sweet spot for the single-pass Light flow.
light_model.recommendedNote: Sonnet 4.6 — Light is a single call, so Opus rarely earns its cost here.

light_max_tokens.label: Max output tokens (Light)
light_max_tokens.help: Ceiling on the dossier the Light engine may generate in one call.
light_max_tokens.tradeoff: Typical output is ~25K. Higher gives headroom so dense HOT dossiers don't truncate, but raises worst-case cost. Must stay above the thinking budget if thinking is on.
light_max_tokens.recommendedNote: 40K — comfortable headroom over the ~25K typical output without overspending.

light_web_search_max_uses.label: Web search budget (Light)
light_web_search_max_uses.help: How many web searches the Light engine may run per dossier.
light_web_search_max_uses.tradeoff: More searches = more evidence sources, but each adds ~1 min of latency, and a lint-retry doubles that. 6 restores pre-tuning depth while staying inside the 15-min Job ceiling.
light_web_search_max_uses.recommendedNote: 6 — the shipped default of 4 was a latency cut, not a quality choice. 6 is noticeably richer and still safe.

light_thinking_enabled.label: Extended thinking (Light)
light_thinking_enabled.help: Give the Light model a private reasoning budget before it writes the dossier.
light_thinking_enabled.tradeoff: Improves scoring nuance on borderline HOT/WARM leads, but adds latency and billed thinking tokens.
light_thinking_enabled.recommendedNote: Off by default. Turn on (with ~8000 budget) when you want deeper scoring and can absorb the extra cost.

light_thinking_budget.label: Thinking budget (Light)
light_thinking_budget.help: Reasoning-token budget when extended thinking is on (0 or >=1024).
light_thinking_budget.tradeoff: Bigger budget = deeper reasoning, more cost. Must be at least 1024 and well below Max output tokens.
light_thinking_budget.recommendedNote: 8000 is a solid starting point when thinking is enabled.

heavy_subagent_model.label: Subagent model (Heavy)
heavy_subagent_model.help: Model for the 4 parallel research subagents (Tech, Compliance, Org, Behavioral).
heavy_subagent_model.tradeoff: Opus on all 4 legs blows the token budget without a commensurate quality lift. Sonnet is the right call for the research legs.
heavy_subagent_model.recommendedNote: Sonnet 4.6 — keep Opus for the parent synthesis only.

heavy_parent_model.label: Parent synthesis model (Heavy)
heavy_parent_model.help: Model that consolidates the 4 fragments into the final dossier.
heavy_parent_model.tradeoff: This is the one place Opus reliably pays off — the richest full_dossier_markdown and the most nuanced scoring. Costs more per Heavy dossier.
heavy_parent_model.recommendedNote: Opus 4.8 — a defensible upgrade for the consolidation pass when cost permits.

heavy_subagent_max_tokens.label: Subagent max tokens (Heavy)
heavy_subagent_max_tokens.tradeoff: Fragments are ~3-5K typical. Raising it helps dense HOT targets (active speaker, big org) avoid truncation, at modest extra cost.
heavy_subagent_max_tokens.recommendedNote: 10K — headroom for high-signal prospects without overspending the 4 legs.

heavy_parent_max_tokens.label: Parent max tokens (Heavy)
heavy_parent_max_tokens.help: Output ceiling for the parent consolidation call.
heavy_parent_max_tokens.tradeoff: The parent must emit the 8-15K char Tab 2 markdown plus the full JSON. 64K is the Sonnet ceiling and a safety cap, not a target — the model only writes what it needs.
heavy_parent_max_tokens.recommendedNote: Leave at 64K — it is a ceiling, not a spend target.

heavy_subagent_web_search_max_uses.label: Subagent web search budget (Heavy)
heavy_subagent_web_search_max_uses.help: Web searches allowed per research subagent.
heavy_subagent_web_search_max_uses.tradeoff: Across 4 subagents this is up to ~40 searches. Pushing to 12 risks the per-subagent 600s timeout firing.
heavy_subagent_web_search_max_uses.recommendedNote: 10 — leaves headroom so an occasional 11th search dip stays inside the timeout.

heavy_subagent_timeout_s.label: Subagent timeout (Heavy)
heavy_subagent_timeout_s.help: Hard ceiling each research subagent may run before it is cut off.
heavy_subagent_timeout_s.tradeoff: Raising it lets slow subagents finish, but fan-out + parent synthesis + render must all fit inside Catalyst's 15-min Job ceiling. Above ~700s you risk the whole job timing out.
heavy_subagent_timeout_s.recommendedNote: 600s — already tuned to fit the 15-min ceiling. Change only with a specific reason.

heavy_parent_thinking_enabled.label: Extended thinking — parent (Heavy)
heavy_parent_thinking_enabled.help: Give the parent synthesis a private reasoning budget before it writes the dossier.
heavy_parent_thinking_enabled.tradeoff: Deepens scoring and narrative coherence on borderline leads, but adds latency (watch the 15-min ceiling) and billed thinking tokens.
heavy_parent_thinking_enabled.recommendedNote: Off by default. Turn on (with ~8000 budget) for the richest scoring when the job has time to spare.

heavy_parent_thinking_budget.label: Thinking budget — parent (Heavy)
heavy_parent_thinking_budget.help: Reasoning-token budget for the parent when thinking is on (0 or >=1024).
heavy_parent_thinking_budget.tradeoff: Bigger budget = deeper reasoning, more cost and time. Must be at least 1024 and well below Parent max tokens.
heavy_parent_thinking_budget.recommendedNote: 8000 is a solid starting point when parent thinking is enabled.

rr_max_bulk_profiles_light.label: RocketReach profile depth (Light)
rr_max_bulk_profiles_light.help: How many RocketReach profiles the Light baseline enrichment pulls.
rr_max_bulk_profiles_light.tradeoff: More profiles = deeper firmographic/contact data feeding synthesis, but uses more RocketReach API budget.
rr_max_bulk_profiles_light.recommendedNote: 15 — modest depth bump over the default of 10.

rr_max_bulk_profiles_heavy.label: RocketReach profile depth (Heavy)
rr_max_bulk_profiles_heavy.help: How many RocketReach profiles the Heavy baseline enrichment pulls.
rr_max_bulk_profiles_heavy.tradeoff: More profiles = richer enrichment shared across the 4 subagents, but uses more RocketReach API budget.
rr_max_bulk_profiles_heavy.recommendedNote: 20 — already a generous depth for Heavy.

light_lint_retry_max.label: Lint retry limit (Light)
light_lint_retry_max.help: How many times Light re-runs synthesis when the depth-lint gate flags a shallow HOT/WARM dossier.
light_lint_retry_max.tradeoff: More retries raise the odds of a full-depth dossier but cost extra tokens and time.
light_lint_retry_max.recommendedNote: 1 — the shipped behavior; a single retry catches most shallow misses.

heavy_lint_retry_max.label: Lint retry limit (Heavy)
heavy_lint_retry_max.help: How many times Heavy re-runs synthesis when the depth-lint gate flags a shallow dossier (today: never).
heavy_lint_retry_max.tradeoff: Heavy already spends a large token budget on fan-out, so a retry is expensive and eats into the 15-min ceiling — but a single retry meaningfully improves depth on shallow HOT/WARM dossiers.
heavy_lint_retry_max.recommendedNote: 1 — let Heavy retry once; it currently never does.

light_lint_soft_tolerance.label: Soft-warning tolerance (Light)
light_lint_soft_tolerance.help: How many isolated empty cells (an Unknown field, an empty budget chart) a COLD/COOL Light dossier may have before it is marked partial ('saved with warnings'). HOT/WARM stay strict — any empty cell flags partial there.
light_lint_soft_tolerance.tradeoff: Higher = fewer noisy warnings on legitimately-sparse low-tier leads, but a genuinely thin COLD/COOL dossier may ship as 'succeeded'. Hard failures (missing section, zero sources) and RocketReach gaps still flag partial at every tier.
light_lint_soft_tolerance.recommendedNote: 4 — absorbs the handful of Unknown cells common on sparse COLD/COOL prospects without masking real thinness.

heavy_lint_soft_tolerance.label: Soft-warning tolerance (Heavy)
heavy_lint_soft_tolerance.help: How many isolated empty cells a COLD/COOL Heavy dossier may have before it is marked partial ('saved with warnings'). HOT/WARM stay strict.
heavy_lint_soft_tolerance.tradeoff: Higher = fewer noisy warnings on sparse low-tier leads; hard failures, missing fan-out subagents, and RocketReach gaps still flag partial at every tier.
heavy_lint_soft_tolerance.recommendedNote: 4 — matches Light; absorbs sparse-prospect Unknown cells without masking real thinness.

heavy_parent_payload_max_chars.label: Parent context cap (Heavy)
heavy_parent_payload_max_chars.help: Max characters of combined fragments + RR + preflight fed into the parent consolidation call before trimming.
heavy_parent_payload_max_chars.tradeoff: Higher = more of the 4 fragments survive into synthesis (richer), but raises parent input tokens and must fit the parent's output budget.
heavy_parent_payload_max_chars.recommendedNote: 80K — balanced; raise only if fragments are visibly getting trimmed.

heavy_subagent_rr_trim_max_chars.label: Subagent input cap (Heavy)
heavy_subagent_rr_trim_max_chars.help: Max characters of the per-lane input payload (intake + preflight + lane-relevant RocketReach slice) passed to each subagent before trimming.
heavy_subagent_rr_trim_max_chars.tradeoff: Higher = each subagent sees more raw context, at the cost of more input tokens per leg (x4).
heavy_subagent_rr_trim_max_chars.recommendedNote: 12K — enough for the lane-relevant slice without bloating the 4 legs.

light_render_timeout_s.label: HTML render timeout (Light)
light_render_timeout_s.help: Subprocess timeout for rendering the dossier HTML.
light_render_timeout_s.tradeoff: Reliability knob, not a richness lever. Too low can fail a valid render; too high wastes the Job budget on a hung render.
light_render_timeout_s.recommendedNote: 120s — leave unless renders are timing out.

light_auto_resume_enabled.label: Auto checkpoint & resume (Light)
light_auto_resume_enabled.help: Persist the completed dossier to Stratus before render so a render/upload failure or a 15-min Job kill is recovered by re-rendering — never a second synthesis call.
light_auto_resume_enabled.tradeoff: Off reverts to the original single-job behavior (a failure after synthesis loses the call's tokens). On adds one small Stratus write per run and may use a second cheap Catalyst job, but guarantees the synthesis tokens are never wasted.
light_auto_resume_enabled.recommendedNote: Leave on — Light is the default path, so this protects most runs.

light_resume_max_attempts.label: Max resume attempts (Light)
light_resume_max_attempts.help: How many resume jobs may be dispatched for one Light request before it is marked failed.
light_resume_max_attempts.tradeoff: Higher tolerates more transient render/upload failures; too high risks looping on a genuinely broken render.
light_resume_max_attempts.recommendedNote: 4 — render-only retries are cheap, so extra headroom rides out a transient stall at no token cost.

heavy_render_timeout_s.label: HTML render timeout (Heavy)
heavy_render_timeout_s.help: Subprocess timeout for rendering the dossier HTML.
heavy_render_timeout_s.tradeoff: Reliability knob, not a richness lever. Heavy dossiers are larger, hence the higher default.
heavy_render_timeout_s.recommendedNote: 180s — leave unless renders are timing out.

heavy_auto_resume_enabled.label: Auto checkpoint & resume (Heavy)
heavy_auto_resume_enabled.help: Master toggle. When on, Heavy persists the fan-out fragments to Stratus the instant they complete and a stalled/deferred job is finished by a fresh resume job — parent synthesis → render → upload — without re-running the 4 research subagents.
heavy_auto_resume_enabled.tradeoff: Off reverts to the original single-job behavior (a 900s kill loses ALL fan-out + parent tokens). On adds one Stratus write per run and may use a second Catalyst job, but guarantees spent Anthropic tokens are never wasted.
heavy_auto_resume_enabled.recommendedNote: Leave on — this is the whole point of the feature.

heavy_synthesis_deadline_s.label: Synthesis deferral deadline (Heavy)
heavy_synthesis_deadline_s.help: Wall-clock elapsed (from job start) past which the job will NOT start parent synthesis in-process — it persists the fan-out checkpoint and hands synthesis to a fresh resume job instead. Protects already-spent subagent tokens from a 15-min Job kill.
heavy_synthesis_deadline_s.tradeoff: Lower defers sooner, so more runs split into a second (cheap) job even when they'd have finished — extra Catalyst compute, never wasted tokens. Higher means fewer deferrals but risks parent+render running past 900s. 660s leaves ~240s for parent synthesis + render + upload inside the 900s ceiling.
heavy_synthesis_deadline_s.recommendedNote: 660s — defers only when the fan-out genuinely ran long, leaving comfortable headroom for synthesis+render.

heavy_parent_timeout_s.label: Parent synthesis timeout (Heavy)
heavy_parent_timeout_s.help: Hard ceiling on the parent consolidation call. On timeout the fan-out checkpoint already exists, so a resume job retries parent synthesis (degraded, if enabled) — no subagent tokens re-spent.
heavy_parent_timeout_s.tradeoff: Too low can cut off a legitimately long parent stream; too high lets a stuck parent eat the remaining job budget. 240s matches the headroom left by the 660s deferral deadline.
heavy_parent_timeout_s.recommendedNote: 240s — aligned with the deferral deadline so parent+render fit the 900s ceiling.

heavy_resume_max_attempts.label: Max resume attempts (Heavy)
heavy_resume_max_attempts.help: How many resume jobs may be dispatched for one request before it is marked failed. Prevents an infinite resume loop when parent synthesis keeps timing out. Resumes after the first defer to the 15-min stale-sweep, so a higher cap spreads retries across ~15 min each rather than clustering them.
heavy_resume_max_attempts.tradeoff: Higher rides out a longer API-degradation window (retries are spaced ~15 min apart and re-spend zero research tokens); too high risks burning parent-synthesis tokens repeatedly on a genuinely broken request.
heavy_resume_max_attempts.recommendedNote: 4 — one fast deferral plus three sweep-spaced retries cover a ~45-min slow spell while still terminating a truly broken request.

heavy_resume_parent_degraded.label: Degrade parent on resume retry (Heavy)
heavy_resume_parent_degraded.help: When a resume is triggered specifically by a parent-synthesis timeout, run the retried parent with extended thinking disabled (faster) — without reducing max_tokens, so the dossier is never truncated.
heavy_resume_parent_degraded.tradeoff: Slightly less reasoning depth on the retried run, but a higher chance the resume completes in time. Only affects timeout-triggered resumes, not the normal deferral resume.
heavy_resume_parent_degraded.recommendedNote: On — a finished dossier (thinking off) beats another timeout.

heavy_synthesis_mode.label: Synthesis mode (Heavy)
heavy_synthesis_mode.help: How the parent dossier is synthesized. 'auto' (default) decides per run: it shards into a spine + parallel section-calls + a narrative reduce ONLY when a single serial monolithic parent call (plus a possible lint retry) wouldn't fit the 900s Job ceiling at the account's throughput — otherwise it uses the cheaper monolithic call. 'sharded' always parallelizes; 'monolithic' is the original single call.
heavy_synthesis_mode.tradeoff: auto = hands-off and adapts to the account (slow account / rich dossier -> sharded automatically; fast account / small dossier -> monolithic, no token premium). 'sharded' maximizes richness headroom at a modest token premium on every run; 'monolithic' minimizes tokens but can't exceed what one serial call fits in 900s. Checkpoint-resume remains the automatic safety net under all three.
heavy_synthesis_mode.recommendedNote: auto — no tuning needed; it applies sharding only when the dossier wouldn't otherwise fit.

heavy_spine_max_tokens.label: Spine max output tokens (Heavy sharded)
heavy_spine_max_tokens.help: Output ceiling for the spine call (scoring + executive_brief + meta) when sharded synthesis is on. The spine is small and runs first to fix the tier and warm the prompt cache.
heavy_spine_max_tokens.tradeoff: The spine is the coherence anchor; too low truncates scoring/scenarios. 6000 is comfortable headroom over the ~2-4K it actually needs.
heavy_spine_max_tokens.recommendedNote: 6000 — only matters when sharded synthesis is enabled.

heavy_shard_max_tokens.label: Per-shard max output tokens (Heavy sharded)
heavy_shard_max_tokens.help: Output ceiling for EACH section shard (8 shards: org, tech, compliance, signals, riskprep, playbook, momdata, quality) when sharded synthesis is on. Higher = richer sections; this is the main richness lever for sharded mode.
heavy_shard_max_tokens.tradeoff: Each shard runs in parallel, so raising this grows per-section depth without extending the others. Worst-case shard wall-clock = this / account-throughput; keep it under heavy_shard_timeout_s × throughput. 12000 leaves headroom so the densest shards (signals, mom-test data) don't truncate.
heavy_shard_max_tokens.recommendedNote: 12000 — covers the densest sections without truncating; shards run concurrently so the cost is tokens, not much wall-clock.

heavy_narrative_max_tokens.label: Narrative max output tokens (Heavy sharded)
heavy_narrative_max_tokens.help: Output ceiling for the narrative reduce that writes full_dossier_markdown (the 8-15K-char Tab 2 prose, returned as raw markdown) over the assembled structured dossier when sharded synthesis is on.
heavy_narrative_max_tokens.tradeoff: full_dossier_markdown targets 8000-15000 chars (~2.5-4K tokens). The narrative is RAW markdown, so a max_tokens stop is non-fatal (it just shortens the prose). This runs serially after the shards, so higher adds wall-clock directly.
heavy_narrative_max_tokens.recommendedNote: 8000 — covers the full 15-section narrative; truncation here only trims length, never breaks parsing.

heavy_shard_timeout_s.label: Per-shard timeout (Heavy sharded)
heavy_shard_timeout_s.help: Wall-clock cap on each parallel section shard. A shard that exceeds it is dropped and its section renders empty (partial), rather than failing the whole dossier — same partial-tolerance as the fan-out subagents.
heavy_shard_timeout_s.tradeoff: Lower fails slow shards faster (more partials); higher tolerates slow sections but enlarges the synthesis budget the pre-synthesis guard reserves. 300s comfortably covers a 9000-token shard at typical throughput.
heavy_shard_timeout_s.recommendedNote: 300s — leave unless shards frequently drop sections.

heavy_sharded_fallback_on_error.label: Fall back to monolithic on sharded error (Heavy)
heavy_sharded_fallback_on_error.help: If sharded synthesis raises an error (e.g. the spine or narrative call fails), run the monolithic single-call parent instead before failing the request. A safety net while the sharded path is being proven.
heavy_sharded_fallback_on_error.tradeoff: On = a sharding bug degrades gracefully to the proven path (costs the already-spent shard tokens plus a full monolithic run). Off = a sharded error fails the request, surfacing the bug loudly.
heavy_sharded_fallback_on_error.recommendedNote: On — graceful degradation while sharded synthesis is new.

preflight_timeout_s.label: Preflight probe timeout
preflight_timeout_s.help: Per-probe timeout for the free OSINT preflight collector.
preflight_timeout_s.tradeoff: Reliability knob. Higher waits longer on slow probes; lower drops more signals to keep the pipeline moving.
preflight_timeout_s.recommendedNote: 10s — leave unless preflight signals are frequently dropping.

rocketreach_timeout_s.label: RocketReach request timeout
rocketreach_timeout_s.help: Per-request timeout for RocketReach API calls.
rocketreach_timeout_s.tradeoff: Reliability knob. Higher tolerates slow RR responses; lower fails faster but may lose enrichment.
rocketreach_timeout_s.recommendedNote: 20s — leave unless RR calls are timing out.

clip_verdict_insight.label: Stored verdict_insight cap
clip_verdict_insight.help: Max characters stored in the leads.verdict_insight column (the rendered HTML in storage is uncapped).
clip_verdict_insight.tradeoff: Affects the stored/searchable field only, not the rendered dossier. The API clamps this to the real column width so it can't cause a silent DB truncation.
clip_verdict_insight.recommendedNote: 10000 — leave; this is a storage cap, not a richness lever.

clip_executive_brief.label: Stored executive_brief cap
clip_executive_brief.help: Max characters stored in the leads.executive_brief column.
clip_executive_brief.tradeoff: Affects the stored/searchable field only, not the rendered dossier. Clamped to the real column width by the API.
clip_executive_brief.recommendedNote: 10000 — leave; storage cap, not a richness lever.

clip_demo_playbook.label: Stored demo_playbook cap
clip_demo_playbook.help: Max characters stored in the leads.demo_playbook column.
clip_demo_playbook.tradeoff: Affects the stored/searchable field only, not the rendered dossier. Clamped to the real column width by the API.
clip_demo_playbook.recommendedNote: 10000 — leave; storage cap, not a richness lever.
